title: Secure Our Ports Act of 2025
stage_label: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
plain_summary: The Secure Our Ports Act of 2025 stops American ports from working with companies that are owned by China, Russia, North Korea, or Iran. This law applies to ports that have special security rules and need safety plans. Port owners cannot let these foreign companies lease, own, or run their port facilities. The goal is to keep America's important shipping ports safe from countries that might be enemies.
last_action_text: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Secure Our Ports Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Prohibition on Certain Contracts for Port Operation and Management
sections.text: This section adds new rules to existing federal shipping laws. The new rule says that anyone who owns or runs a port facility that needs a security plan cannot make contracts with certain foreign companies. They cannot make contracts to let these companies own, lease, or operate their port facilities. The banned companies are those owned by the governments of China, Russia, North Korea, or Iran. The ban also applies to any foreign company where any part of it is owned by one of these four countries. The terms facility and owner or operator have the same meanings as defined elsewhere in federal shipping law. This new rule will be added to the official list of shipping laws as section seventy thousand fifteen.
